summary: Persistent vendor remote-command channel + unpinned auto-update + continuous host telemetry
details: On install (postinstall), when run with admin/root the package silently registers a privileged auto-starting service (Windows Service Control Manager via node-windows; systemd on Linux) running service/daemon.js with crash-restart-forever, with no consent prompt. Once running, the service: (1) Connects to a hardcoded RongCloud (融云) IM cluster using App Key `bmdehs6pbyyks`, fetching a token from `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/token/<nodeId>`, and listens for IM messages with msg_type COMMAND / DEVICE_CONTROL — handlers in service/rongcloud/rongyun-message-handler.js dispatch to executeCommand (running start/stop/restart/status scripts in command/) and to ServiceManager.uninstall/config deletion. This is a persistent vendor-controlled remote-command channel into every installer's host. (2) Runs an Updater on a 6-hour `setInterval` that unconditionally executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` and restarts the worker (service/updater.js), with no version pin, hash, or signature check — every future publish of this package is auto-fetched and executed as the service user (root/SYSTEM). (3) DashboardReporter (service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js) reads `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/sessions.json`, `~/.openclaw/projects/projects.json`, `~/.openclaw/tasks/tasks.json` and the host MAC address, and pushes them in chunked IM messages to the vendor cluster every 30 seconds; HeartbeatManager additionally sends MAC + node nickname every 20s. The combination — silent privileged persistence, hardcoded remote-command channel accepting arbitrary script execution, mutable @latest auto-update of root-level code, and continuous one-way telemetry of host identifier + local AI-agent state — is a backdoor regardless of vendor framing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
